🇯🇵 Comment TOKYO and I’ll send you a free Tokyo Travel Guide + 5 day itinerary including how to get there, where to stay, what to do and how to buy tickets.

Get 10% off Japan train tickets and activities on Klook by using code MINYWANDER (10% for new users and over $50 spend, 3% for existing users).

🇯🇵 10 things to do in Tokyo ⬇️

There’s no city quite like Tokyo. From towering skyscrapers to bustling markets, serene temples to glitzy neon signs, there is something for everyone.

🌤️ Shibuya Sky - observation deck located by Shibuya Scramble Crossing. 
💫 TeamLab Planets or Borderless - interactive digital art museums with galleries of immersive installations.
🚶‍♀️ Shibuya Scramble Crossing - fun place to people-watch as it is the largest pedestrian crossing. Located in front of Shibuya Station - estimated 1000-2500 people crossing at once.
🗼 Tokyo Tower - symbol of Japan’s economic growth. Used to be the tallest building in Tokyo until Tokyo Skytree was built in 2012.
🐟 Tsukiji Outer Market - market containing seafood, vegetables, traditional food materials. It is touristy and expensive, but worth checking out at least once.
🗻 Mt. Fuji Day Trip - take the train to Lake Kawaguchiko for sights of Mt. Fuji. Take caution as Mt. Fuji is only visible on clear days!
🏮 Senso-ji Temple - in the historic Asakusa district, this is Tokyo’s oldest temple. 
⛩️ Meiji Jingu Shrine - this shrine is a peaceful retreat in a forested area with colorful display of kazaridaru or sake barrels. 
🛍️ Harajuku - trendy district with Takeshita Street at its heart, lined with vibrant fashion stores, colorful boutiques, quirky shops, and cafes (and crepe stores). 
🍣 Sushi Omakase - chef curated course meal made fresh in front of you, where every bite is a masterpiece. 

📌 Save this post for your Tokyo trip!
👉🏻 Follow @minywander for more travel inspo and tips!

transcript: If you're going to visit Tokyo for the first time, you need to plan strategically to make the most of your time. The best way to experience the city is by grouping your days by neighborhoods so you're not wasting time getting around. Here's my five-day Tokyo itinerary that maximizes your time. On day one, arrive and explore Shinjuku. Walk through buzzing streets, towering skyscrapers, and check out the Yokocho, which are tiny alleyways filled with bars and izakayas. Day two, start at Meiji Jingu Shrine and then explore Harajuku and Omotesando. Then head up to Shibuya Sky in the afternoon for panoramic views of Tokyo from an open air deck. You can see the famous Shibuya scramble crossing from up here. On day three, start your morning at Asakusa at the oldest temple of Tokyo, Sensoji Temple. Walk through Nakamise Street in front of the temple, then grab lunch at Ameyoko Market in Ueno, and then check out the electric town of Akihabara in the evening. Day four, start your morning at Tsukiji Market, and then check out one of the Team Labs exhibitions, either Team Labs Planets or Borderless. Admire the Tokyo Tower, and then head to Tokyo Station or Ginza for the evening. On day five, you'll venture out of the city and do a day trip to Kawaguchi Cove for Mount Fuji views. If you're curious about where to stay and how to navigate through Tokyo throughout this itinerary, comment Tokyo and I'll send you the link to the full itinerary with more details.
